clinical_history: 85-year-old female with asymptomatic bradycardia. What does this ECG show?
diagnosis: This ECG shows borderline sinus tachycardia (about 105/min) and a ventricular rate of about 35/min, yielding 3:1 "advanced" AV block. There is an intraventricular conduction delay with right  bundle branch block and left anterior fascicular block (QRS = 0.13 second, with marked left axis deviation). The conducting P ("third) wave has a prolonged PR interval of about 230 ms and is constant.  Left atrial abnormality and left ventricular hypertrophy are noted.~~~~The constancy of the conducted PR intervals rules out AV Wenckebach. The consistent sinus P waves (at a rate close to 105 bpm) rules of out sick sinus syndrome/SA exit block. Complete heart block is excluded as every 3rd P wave is conducted. This pattern is referred to as advanced second degree AV block. The finding of 3:1 block with bifascicular block is consistent with advanced infranodal disease and is an indication for permanent pacemaker even without overt symptoms.~~~~~~
